---
title: "get_franchise_performance"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd)

# `get_franchise_performance()`

Franchise Performance Rollup

## Usage

```r
get_franchise_performance(
  franchise_title,
  platform_name = "netflix",
  country_name = "United States",
  start_date,
  end_date,
  flix_type = "movies",
  token = "FLIX_PATROL"
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `franchise_title` | Character. The franchise name (e.g., "Marvel", "Star Wars"). |
| `platform_name` | Character. Platform name. |
| `country_name` | Character. Country name. |
| `start_date` | Date/Character. Start of the range. |
| `end_date` | Date/Character. End of the range. |
| `flix_type` | Character. Content type (default '"movies"'). |
| `token` | Character. API environment variable name. |

## Value

A tibble with columns 'title', 'days_on_chart', 'best_rank', 'avg_rank',
plus a 'franchise' column.

## Description

Looks up all titles in a franchise, then aggregates their Top 10 appearances
across a date range into a single summary table.
